entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: An important cereal crop grown in tropical and temperate environments.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental factor that influences the flowering responses of sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: The length of daylight, an environmental factor influencing the flowering responses of sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: PHOTOTHERMAL FLOWERING RESPONSES
entities.description: The genotypic responses of sorghum flowering to the combined effects of temperature and photoperiod.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: One of the countries where sorghum genotypes were grown for the study.

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: One of the countries where sorghum genotypes were grown for the study.

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: One of the countries where sorghum genotypes were grown for the study.

entities.name: TIMES FROM SOWING TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The duration from the date of sowing until the plant reaches the flowering stage.

entities.name: PHOTOTHERMAL MODELS
entities.description: Models used to quantify the responsiveness of flowering time to temperature and photoperiod.

entities.name: MULTIPLICATIVE RATE PHOTOTHERMAL MODEL
entities.description: A specific photothermal model used to accurately predict the time from sowing to flowering.

entities.name: MINIMUM TIME TO FLOWER
entities.description: The shortest possible time a genotype takes to flower, denoted as Fm.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The degree to which flowering is affected by photoperiod, determining broad adaptation to latitude.

entities.name: CRITICAL PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: A specific photoperiod threshold (Pc) used to measure photoperiod sensitivity.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD-SENSITIVITY SLOPE
entities.description: The slope (Ps) used to measure the rate of change in flowering response to photoperiod.

entities.name: BASE TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature threshold (Tb) below which plant development ceases.

entities.name: LATITUDE
entities.description: A geographic factor related to daylength that influences broad adaptation of sorghum.

entities.name: HUMID AND SUB-HUMID TROPICS
entities.description: Specific environmental ranges within latitudes where minimum time to flower determines adaptation.

entities.name: ADAPTATION TO ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The phenological basis of how sorghum genotypes adjust to diverse environments.
